Scenario.title: C0027: Spearphishing-Driven Cyberattack on Energy Sector Infrastructure
Scenario.overview: Energy Intermediate A sophisticated cyberattack targeting an energy sector organization begins with a spearphishing campaign leveraging a zero-day vulnerability in Microsoft Office. The attackers execute malicious macros to establish persistence, escalate privileges, and ultimately manipulate industrial control systems to disrupt critical infrastructure.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2022-26904 [CWE-502]: Apache Log4j2 JNDI features used in configuration...
stage.description: An attacker sends a tailored phishing email containing a malicious Microsoft Office document to a high-value employee in the energy sector. When opened, the document exploits a zero-day vulnerability (CVE-2022-26904) to execute arbitrary code, granting initial access to the victim's machine.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2021-30869 [CWE-502]: Apache Log4j2 JNDI features used in configuration...
stage.description: The embedded macro in the malicious document triggers execution of a Cobalt Strike beacon payload, establishing a reverse shell connection to the attacker-controlled command-and-control server.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: A scheduled task is created in Windows Task Scheduler to periodically execute the attacker's payload, ensuring continued access to the compromised system even after reboot.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: The attacker exploits a known vulnerability in an unpatched Apache Struts service to escalate privileges to administrator-level access, enabling broader network traversal.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.description: Malicious payloads are encoded using PowerShell obfuscation techniques to bypass signature-based detection mechanisms on endpoint security platforms.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attacker modifies SCADA system parameters to alter turbine speeds and valve positions in the energy grid, causing physical damage to infrastructure and triggering widespread power outages.

Scenario.summary: The C0027 campaign exploited human factors and software vulnerabilities to infiltrate an energy provider's network, escalating privileges and ultimately disrupting industrial control systems. This multi-stage attack highlights the risks of unpatched systems and social engineering in critical infrastructure sectors.
